sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचकाम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः । महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  रजोगुणसे उत्पन्न हुआ यह काम ही क्रोध है ।  यह बहुत खानेवाला और महापापी है ।  इस विषयमें तू इसको ही वैरी जान ॥
englishTranslation: Krishna said: Desire, born of rajas, turns into anger. It is all-consuming and deeply destructive. Know it as your enemy here.
essence: Desire is not harmless; it mutates into the force that ruins judgment.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Desire, born from restlessness, turns into anger and destroys clarity. Krishna says to recognize it as an enemy before it takes over.
simpleInsight: You can catch the fire early, before it becomes a blaze that controls your choices.
detailedMeaning: Arjuna has asked why people act against their own judgment. Krishna answers on the battlefield: the real enemy is not outside, but the force of desire rising into anger and blinding discernment. This verse names it so Arjuna can fight it first.
modernRelevance: You open a message, feel ignored, and the irritation hardens into a cutting reply. The first spark looked harmless; the full fire changes everything.
translationLiteral: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Lord said) / कामः (desire) / एषः (this) / क्रोधः (anger) / एषः (this) / रजोगुणसमुद्भवः (born from the quality of passion) / महाशनः (all-devouring) / महापाप्मा (greatly destructive) / विद्धि (know) / एनम् (this) / इह (here) / वैरिणम् (as an enemy)
insight: It shifts the mind from blaming others to spotting the inner impulse that distorts action.

quoteVariants.text: Desire becomes anger. Treat it as the enemy.
Desire devours. Know it as enemy.
That urge can turn into anger before you notice.
Name desire before it names you.
